strengths: Full Ten-Step Cycle (A->B->C->D) executes end-to-end with unbroken context chain: spec_id -> task_list -> branch_name -> commit_sha -> pr_url -> archive_path
Level classification correctly routes: Level 1 skips Phase A, Level 3 triggers full spec + tasks pipeline
TDD double-insurance (Method A subagent constraint + Method B session pre-hook) activates correctly for both bugfix and feature scenarios
branch-manager Worktree/Branch mode scoring correctly triggers Worktree mode (score>=3) for cross-module changes and Branch mode (score<3) for simple fixes
agent-router correctly assigns backend-architect, mobile-developer, and qa-engineer by module and technology stack
strategic-commit-orchestrator groups commits semantically by module (feat(api), feat(mobile), feat(db)) rather than by file type
branch-finisher error recovery flow: detects failures, offers retry (not just abort), re-runs full validation, then proceeds to Phase C
Phase D atomic closure: D.1 (UPMv2-STATE update) and D.2 (openspec-archive) execute as a unit with rollback guarantee
openspec-archive correctly targets openspec/archive/ (project-scoped) and not standards/openspec/archive/ (shared submodule)
state-scanner post-closure reports clean state with no active specs in changes/ directory
recommendations: Add eval for concurrent spec conflict detection: two specs starting simultaneously on overlapping modules
Add eval for partial Phase A completion: spec-drafter fails mid-write, verify recovery path
Add eval for Worktree cleanup: verify .worktrees/ directories are removed after integration branch merge
Add eval for UPM stateToken collision: two concurrent closures writing to openspec/project.md simultaneously
Add eval for multi-iteration brainstorm: user requests exploration across 2+ sessions before committing to spec
Expand error-handling category: test commit-msg-generator fallback when diff exceeds context limit
